items.title: MARKET PULSE PM JULY 3, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia ended higher on Friday, led by a rebound in banking stocks as sentiment improved after softer-than-expected US labour market data. Meanwhile, the ringgit rose 4.0670. In the crypto market, Bitcoin rose to RM250,365.

items.title: Foreign beggar activities in KL linked to organised crime syndicates, says ministry
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Begging activities involving foreigners, especially children, are believed to be linked to organised crime syndicates, the Home Ministry said. In a written reply published on the Parliament portal, the ministry said the Immigration Department conducted 836 enforcement operations in Kuala Lumpur and its surrounding areas between 2025 and May 31 this year. To enhance enforcement, the ministry said the Immigration Department has stepped up integrated operations with the police and Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL), while improving intelligence sharing to map out syndicate networks.

items.title: Johor polls: Housing, environment top PSM manifesto for Skudai
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Parti Sosialis Malaysia (PSM) has unveiled its manifesto for the Skudai seat in the Johor polls, focusing on affordable housing, environmental sustainability and youth employment. On housing, PSM pledged to push for genuinely affordable homes and rental controls aligned with the incomes of ordinary citizens, alongside more urban transit housing for young families and the working class. To address this, PSM proposed building more People's Housing Project (PPR) flats for rent and repurposing abandoned commercial buildings into affordable rental units.

items.title: Johor election: Tiram seat sees fierce four-cornered battle
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Tiram state seat, once regarded as a traditional stronghold of Barisan Nasional, has re-emerged as a key battleground in the Johor political landscape following shifting voter sentiment in recent general and state elections. The seat, which fell to Pakatan Harapan in the 2018 general election, was reclaimed by BN in the 2022 Johor election when its candidate Azizul Bachok secured a comfortable majority of 8,098 votes in a seven-cornered contest. In the current state election, BN is fielding veteran politician Senator Datuk Abdul Halim Suleiman, who is also Tebrau Umno division chief, in a bid to retain the seat.

items.title: Ringgit ends higher against US dollar on favourable Asian currency sentiment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit ended higher against the US dollar at the close of the trading week, supported by favourable Asian currency sentiment following the latest United States Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP) data. At 6 pm, the local note appreciated to 4.0690/0735 against the greenback from Thursday's close of 4.0780/0830. Mohd Afzanizam added that Asian currencies performed favourably against the US dollar today.

items.title: Respect Court of Appeal's full judgment on PSR project, NGO tells DAP Youth
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Non-governmental organisation Pertubuhan Jaringan Ekologi dan Iklim (Jedi) has urged Penang DAP Youth to respect the full Court of Appeal judgment on the Penang South Reclamation (PSR) project. Jedi said it accepted the Court of Appeal's decision to dismiss the appeal, but argued that the judgment also contained significant findings on the powers of state planning authorities over reclamation involving the territorial sea and seabed. Jedi said the Court of Appeal's findings raised legitimate questions over the legal authority relied upon to commence reclamation and grant planning permission.

items.title: DBKL shuts 2 foreigner-run premises at Kuala Lumpur Wholesale Market
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) has ordered the immediate closure of two foreigner-operated business premises at the Kuala Lumpur Wholesale Market for operating without licences during a joint enforcement operation. It said inspections covered wholesalers dealing in fruits, vegetables, frozen food and general goods, focusing on premises suspected of operating without licences, failing to display business licences, being operated by foreign nationals, obstructing public walkways, operating without signboard licences and breaching licensing conditions. The two premises were ordered to close immediately for operating without valid business licences and being run by foreign nationals.

items.title: Global stocks climb as US jobs data cools Fed hike fears
items.summary: LONDON/SINGAPORE: Global stocks climbed on Friday after a lukewarm US jobs report softened expectations for an imminent rate hike from the Federal Reserve and regional activity gauges pointed to an economic expansion during June. Germany's DAX index rose 0.1 per cent while the French and UK indices were a touch lower on the day. MSCI's broadest index of world shares rose 0.4 per cent.

items.title: Rebound in banking stocks lifts Bursa Malaysia at close
items.summary: Among heavyweight stocks, Maybank gained six sen to RM10.80, while CIMB added 15 sen to RM7.60, and Tenaga Nasional climbed two sen to RM14.28. As for the active stocks, Velesto added two sen to 28.5 sen, Zetrix AI gained one sen to 79 sen, and Silver Ridge gained four sen to 21 sen. Batu Kawan soared 54 sen to RM21.64, and PPB bagged 21 sen to RM9.54.

items.title: Johor election heats up as Anwar, Zahid and other leaders descend this weekend
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The heat of the Johor election is intensifying as top political leaders descend on the state to attend a series of campaign events and ceramahs this weekend. This will be Anwar's first visit to Johor for the state election campaign since nomination day last Saturday. [[nid:1479665]] PH is going head-to-head against BN in all 56 seats up for grabs in the Johor election.

items.title: Habib Jewels unveils Italian craftsmanship in gold
items.summary: The campaign celebrates the Oro Italia 916 collection, where traditional Italian craftsmanship meets 916 gold to create jewellery that blends timeless elegance with contemporary design. Beyond the aesthetics, Italian jewellery expert Paolo, one of Habib's long-time manufacturing partners from Italy, said every piece begins with uncompromising quality control before the design itself takes shape. Paolo said crafting jewellery in 916 gold presents unique challenges because of the metal's softness compared with lower-purity gold commonly used in Europe.

items.title: Taxi commuters welcome RM10mil reform but call for better digitalisation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Taxi commuters have welcomed the RM10 million National Madani Taxi Reform Programme but said stronger digitalisation, fares and service standards are needed to restore confidence. Commuters interviewed by the New Straits Times said transparent fares, improved customer service and greater adoption of digitalisation remain key to persuading commuters to return to taxis. Wan Adam Wan Amin, 27, from Ampang, said payment methods also remain inconsistent.

items.title: Johor election: PH manifesto just expanding existing programmes, says Hishammuddin
items.summary: SEMBRONG: Sembrong member of Parliament Datuk Seri Hishammuddin Hussein has dismissed Pakatan Harapan's election pledges, saying they do not amount to a manifesto. The former Umno vice-president said the pledges outlined in the PH manifesto were merely increases in the numbers of existing programmes implemented by the Johor government under caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi. Earlier today, PH launched its "Johor Untuk Semua" manifesto as its offer to voters in conjunction with the 16th Johor state election.

items.title: Johor election: PH manifesto offers nothing game-changing, says Zahid
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Some of the pledges announced in Pakatan Harapan's (PH) manifesto have already been implemented by the Johor government under Barisan Nasional. BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said there was nothing "game-changing" in the PH manifesto. [[nid:1479652]] Also present was Johor BN chairman and caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi.

items.title: Customs busts two syndicates, seizes RM2.57 million in illicit liquor and chewing tobacco
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Kuala Lumpur Customs Department busted two syndicates involved in processing illicit liquor and smuggling chewing tobacco, seizing uncustomed goods worth RM2.57 million in a recent special operation. Noraidah Ishak, who is performing the duties of the Kuala Lumpur Customs director, said two foreigners were arrested during Ops Suling, which was conducted from May 11 to 23. She said the operations team raided two warehouses along Jalan Wangsa Utama, Taman Wangsa Permai here, on May 20 and seized 4,987 litres of whisky bearing counterfeit tax stamps.

items.title: Mitra receives 1,340 applications for Madani grant programme in 5 days
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Indian Community Transformation Unit (Mitra) has received 1,340 applications for its Uyarvu Madani Grant Programme within five days, nearing its target of 1,500 applications. Mitra director-general N. Raveendran Nair said the programme, launched on June 29 by Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan, has drawn strong interest from Indian community entrepreneurs seeking financial assistance to expand their businesses. The Uyarvu Madani Grant Programme is one of Mitra's initiatives aimed at enhancing the capacity and competitiveness of Indian community entrepreneurs while supporting long-term business growth.

items.title: DBKL cracks down on Hicks Flats over cleaning, compliance failures
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) has carried out an enforcement operation at Hicks, Bukit Bintang, after a follow-up inspection found continued non-compliance by the building's Joint Management Body (JMB). DBKL said the building management body of Hicks Flats was found to have failed to comply with directives previously issued by the authority. DBKL also issued one compound under Section 47(1)(a) of the Street, Drainage and Building Act 1974, and another under Section 82(5) of the Local Government Act 1976 for failure to comply with cleaning directives.

items.title: Indonesian man jailed 30 years for murdering wife at Sabah market
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: An Indonesian man was sentenced to 30 years' jail and 12 strokes of the rotan by the High Court today for murdering his wife three years ago. Judge Datuk Alexander Siew How Wai imposed the sentence on Jedu Rudu, 45, who admitted to murdering his wife, Suria Woade, 38, also an Indonesian, at a vegetable market in Jalan Pahlawan, Kepayan, on April 8, 2023, between 1pm and 2.21pm. According to the facts of the case, the victim, the accused's estranged wife, was at the Kepayan vegetable market helping her elder sister sell vegetables.

items.title: NRES vows stronger measures after elephant calf's death
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Ministry (NRES) is committed to strengthening mitigation measures to reduce human-wildlife conflict following the death of an elephant calf hit by a vehicle on Jalan Felda Nitar near Mersing, Johor, yesterday. He said human-wildlife conflict is driven by interconnected factors, including growing human and elephant populations. NRES is also developing the Johor Elephant Sanctuary (JES) in phases as a strategic initiative to manage elephant habitats systematically.

items.title: Home Ministry: Foreign begging cases may involve syndicates, 42 detained in KL operations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — Begging activities involving foreigners, especially children, may involve organised crime and are believed to be linked to syndicates, said the Ministry of Home Affairs (KDN). It said from 2025 until May 31 this year, the Immigration Department carried out 836 enforcement operations in Kuala Lumpur and surrounding areas, inspecting 10,609 individuals. The ministry said to enhance enforcement, the Immigration Department has increased integrated operations with the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) and Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL), and improved intelligence sharing and analysis to identify syndicate networks, modus operandi, and begging hotspots.

items.title: Anwar: JS‑SEZ on track with federal-state cooperation, urges leaders not to politicise issue
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — The Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) is progressing according to plan and involves close cooperation between the federal and state governments, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. “This is a federal government project, naturally in collaboration with the state government. The matter of announcements and signing is between the Prime Minister of Malaysia and the Prime Minister of Singapore,” he told reporters after Friday prayers at Masjid As-Sodiqin, Taman Kobena, here today.

items.title: Malaysia, Thailand boost border cooperation following Tak Bai blast
items.summary: RANTAU PANJANG: Malaysia and Thailand have agreed to strengthen operational cooperation along the Sungai Golok border to curb cross-border crime and prevent security spillovers following the recent bomb blast in Tak Bai that injured two Malaysians. General Operations Force (GOF) Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said both countries would enhance direct communication between security commanders on the ground to enable faster responses to border issues. The Thai delegation is introducing the newly established NCCC, which was formed about a year ago, to security agencies in neighbouring countries, including Malaysia.

items.title: MOH overhauls emergency triage system with new 5-tier scale
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Health Ministry (MOH) is revamping the triage system at public hospital emergency departments with the Malaysian Triage Scale (MTS) 2022 to assess patients more accurately and cut treatment delays. Under the new MTS 2022, MOH has upgraded the previous three-tier colour-coded system under the Malaysian Triage Category (2011) to a more refined five-tier scale, ranging from Level 1 (Resuscitation) to Level 5 (Routine). The ministry said the new system enhances assessment precision by dividing the process into Primary Triage (quick first-glance evaluation) and Secondary Triage (detailed assessment with vital signs).

items.title: Zahid urges all parties to avoid old, unrelated issues in Johor campaign
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 3 — Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has reminded all parties to stop playing up old issues unrelated to Johor during the state election campaign period, to ensure a mature and respectful political contest. Ahmad Zahid also denied claims that BN is overconfident in facing the state election, saying the party considers itself the underdog, based on changes in the state’s political landscape. BN is contesting all 56 seats in the 16th Johor state election.

items.title: Man injured after durian tree crashes onto house
items.summary: YAN: A man was injured after a durian tree crashed onto his house in Kampung Permatang Keramat here today. Ku Osman Ku Hamid, who is in his 40s, was trapped inside the upper section of the house after the tree struck the structure. The Kedah Fire and Rescue Department said it received an emergency call at 10.07am and dispatched six firefighters from the Yan station, led by Senior Fire Officer II Bahari Ahmad.

items.title: WWF urges faster wildlife crossings after elephant calf death
items.summary: WWF Malaysia said in a statement that roads have become deadly traps each year for both wildlife and road users. It said solutions including wildlife corridors, eco-friendly tunnels and overpasses should be implemented more quickly. WWF Malaysia said the incident also resulted in serious injuries to a driver and property damage.

items.title: Penang airport expansion records strong progress, project slated for 2028 completion, says exco
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 3 — The expansion of the Penang International Airport is progressing according to schedule, with several key work packages recording encouraging progress and the overall project expected to be completed in 2028. Penang Infrastructure, Transport and Digital Committee chairman Zairil Khir Johari said Work Package One (WP01), comprising the construction of support buildings, utility relocation, infrastructure works and the Remote Digital Virtual Tower (RDVT), had achieved 100 per cent completion. Zairil said the Penang International Airport expansion is among the Federal Government’s strategic initiatives to increase the airport’s annual passenger handling capacity from 6.5 million to 12 million.

items.title: Ringgit faces near-term headwinds, medium-term outlook still positive: Standard Chartered
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit is expected to remain under near-term pressure due to external uncertainties and volatile portfolio flows but its medium-term prospects remain positive, according to Standard Chartered Global Research. The assessment follows Bank Negara Malaysia's Financial Markets Committee's (FMC) latest statement on developments in the foreign exchange market after the ringgit came under renewed pressure against the US dollar. Standard Chartered said the FMC's statement was likely prompted by the ringgit's recent underperformance and the pace of its depreciation.

items.title: Taxi group: New scheme cheaper than leasing, boosts ownership
items.summary: Its president, Kamarudin Mohd Hussain, said the initiative offers a lower daily financing structure compared with the existing leasing system. Kamarudin said about 800 taxi drivers have applied to join the rejuvenation programme, though most are still in the process of obtaining permits from the Land Public Transport Agency. It is simpler and cheaper than existing taxi leasing arrangements, he said.

items.title: Perdana Park returns: Tanjung Aru’s favourite sunset spot reopens this Saturday
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 3 — Perdana Park in Tanjung Aru is making its comeback tomorrow, after a prolonged and sporadic closure that began nearly two years ago. Visitors can access the park daily from 7am to 7pm. DBKK warned that several areas remain off-limits while safety assessments continue.

items.title: BN confident of victory, targets over 40 seats to retain Johor, says Ahmad Maslan
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 3 — Barisan Nasional (BN) is confident of winning the Johor polls and forming the next state government to ensure continuity in the state’s administration. Johor Umno deputy liaison committee chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Maslan said BN was on course to achieve its target of winning more than 40 of the 56 seats in the State Legislative Assembly. He said his confidence was based on his observations and assessment after assisting campaign efforts in 25 of Johor’s 26 parliamentary constituencies.

items.title: Penang economy grows 7.3pct in 2025, driven by manufacturing, services
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Penang's economy expanded 7.3 per cent in 2025, with gross domestic product (GDP) rising to RM130.3 billion from RM121.4 billion in 2024. The Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) said the state's economic growth was driven mainly by the manufacturing and services sectors, which together accounted for about 94 per cent of Penang's GDP. Penang's economy continues to be anchored by these two key sectors, with manufacturing accounting for more than 46 per cent of the economy, while services contribute about 48 per cent.

items.title: Suspended soldier, 3 others plead not guilty to perfume investment scam
items.summary: Puteri Alisa Nabila Muhammad Razali, 23, was jointly charged with Mohammad Nazirul Azri Fairus, 29, Mariam Najwa Mahadi, 27, and Khor Leong Weong, 48. All four claimed trial before Magistrate Faezahnoor Hassan. Deputy public prosecutor Mohamad Fadhly Mohd Zamry asked the court to set bail at RM50,000 for Puteri Alisa Nabila and RM10,000 each for the three other accused, with one surety each.

items.title: MOH overhauls emergency triage system with new five‑tier Malaysian Triage Scale to improve assessments and cut delays
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) is revamping the triage system at public hospital emergency departments with the Malaysian Triage Scale (MTS) 2022 to assess patients more accurately and cut treatment delays. Under the new MTS 2022, MOH has upgraded the previous three-tier colour-coded system under the Malaysian Triage Category (2011) to a more refined five-tier scale, ranging from Level 1 (Resuscitation) to Level 5 (Routine). The Ministry said the new system enhances assessment precision by dividing the process into Primary Triage (quick first-glance evaluation) and Secondary Triage (detailed assessment with vital signs).

items.title: Anwar: US pardon won’t get Jho Low off the hook in Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has maintained that Malaysia will not alter its position on fugitive financier Jho Low, regardless of whether the US government grants him a presidential pardon. Speaking to reporters after Friday prayers at Masjid As-Sodiqin in Cheras, Anwar decoupled Malaysia’s legal stance from any potential clemency in the US, NST reported. Further reports from The Wall Street Journal indicate that Low filed a pardon request in May seeking the dismissal of his US criminal charges.

items.title: Kelantan residents keep crossing into Thailand despite bomb blast
items.summary: RANTAU PANJANG: Residents in Kelantan's border towns continued crossing into southern Thailand as usual today despite a roadside bomb blast that injured two Malaysians earlier this week. A check by the New Straits Times at the Rantau Panjang Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) Complex found traffic flowing normally, with a steady stream of pedestrians and vehicles crossing the Malaysia-Thailand border throughout the morning. Border traffic remained steady four days after a bomb exploded near Tak Bai, Narathiwat, in an attack believed to have targeted a Thai security volunteer vehicle.

items.title: Johor expands education reform to religious schools under ‘Sekolah Rintis Bangsa Johor’ initiative, says MB
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 3 — The Johor government is expanding the Sekolah Rintis Bangsa Johor (SRBJ) initiative to religious education with the establishment of the first Sekolah Agama Rintis Bangsa Johor (SARBJ) this year. Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the initiative is the brainchild of the Regent of Johor, Tunku Mahkota Ismail, to comprehensively transform the state’s education system. “Alhamdulillah, the Sekolah Rintis Bangsa Johor initiative is the Regent of Johor’s brainchild.

items.title: PM receives farewell visit from Chief Statistician Uzir Mahidin ahead of retirement, thanks him for years of service
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today received a farewell courtesy visit from Chief Statistician of Malaysia Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin ahead of his mandatory retirement on July 8. In a Facebook post, the Prime Minister expressed his appreciation for Mohd Uzir’s service and dedication in strengthening the national statistical system, which serves as a key foundation for policymaking and Malaysia’s development planning. Anwar also shared photos of the visit, noting that Mohd Uzir was appointed Chief Statistician on February 14, 2017.

items.title: Court ruling clear, reclamation project to proceed, says Chow
items.summary: Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the appellate court had dismissed the appeal and that was the only fact that should be accepted unless the matter was taken to a higher court. "I believe no party should question the court's decision. The fact remains that the Court of Appeal dismissed the appeal.

items.title: Bursa achieves FTSE4Good ESG top score
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia Bhd has become the first Malaysian public listed company to achieve the highest possible score of 5.0 under the FTSE4Good ESG assessment framework and index methodology. "This is attributed to Bursa Malaysia's enhanced sustainability disclosures across environmental, social and governance (ESG) matters, particularly in climate-related reporting aligned with internationally recognised standards such as IFRS S2," it said in a statement. Its chief executive officer Datuk Fad'l Malaysia said the achievement reflects Bursa Malaysia's progress in strengthening its ESG practices and disclosures.

items.title: Anwar: Azam Baki’s NFCC role independent of MACC post
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — Tan Sri Azam Baki’s membership in the National Anti-Financial Crime Centre (NFCC) Advisory Board has nothing to do with his previous position as the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Anwar said Azam’s appointment to the board falls under the authority of the Yang di-Pertuan Agong and remains valid until 2027. Earlier, the National Financial Crime Prevention Centre (NFCC) clarified that Azam is still a member of its Advisory Board.

items.title: Court of Appeal upholds ruling that DAP’s Chong Chieng Jen defamed Sarawak government
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — The Court of Appeal has unanimously dismissed an appeal by Sarawak DAP chief Chong Chieng Jen, upholding a High Court ruling that he defamed the Sarawak government and the Sarawak Financial Authority (SFA) over claims regarding state funds in 2013. The three-judge panel increased the damages awarded to the state government and the SFA from RM150,000 to RM300,000. Chong said following the decision that he is considering filing an appeal to the Federal Court.

items.title: Five Taiping Prison officers charged over alleged baton attacks on inmates
items.summary: TAIPING, July 3 — Five prison officers were charged in the Magistrate's Court here today with injuring three inmates at Taiping Prison in separate incidents last January. All of the accused — Mohd Hairie Jumri, 40; Saiful Azman Mohamad Ibrahim, 44; Ahmad Rizal Razali, 37; Muhamad Mustakhim Abdul Rahim, 40; and Nor Hafiz Izwan Muhammad Jajam, 31 — pleaded not guilty to the charges. In the court before Magistrate R. Prabakaran, Mohd Hairie and Saiful Azman are jointly charged with intentionally causing hurt to P. Jason Immanuel, 25, using a baton at Hall B, Taiping Prison, Jalan Taming Sari here, between 3pm and 4pm on Jan 17, 2025.

items.title: Shafee tells court Najib’s tax appeal would be moot if LHDN insists on ‘pay first, talk later’
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 3 — Lawyer Tan Sri Shafee Abdullah argued before the Court of Appeal today that failure to stay the Inland Revenue Board’s (LHDN) case against ex-prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak would render his appeal before the Special Commissioners of Income Tax (SCIT) in September “meaningless.” Speaking before a three-member bench chaired by Justice Datuk Alwi Abdul Wahab, Shafee contended that proceeding with the bankruptcy action would be “akin to entering the boxing ring with both hands tied,” Free Malaysia Today reported. The dispute centres on an additional tax assessment of RM1.69 billion for the 2011 to 2017 period, stemming from an alleged RM8 billion donation that Najib maintains was used for political and welfare activities. This hearing follows a November 17 ruling last year, in which the High Court dismissed applications by Najib and his son, Nazifuddin, to stay bankruptcy proceedings over unpaid tax arrears of RM1.69 billion and RM37.6 million, respectively.

items.title: Home minister: Police working with Interpol to locate Na’imah, children in Europe in ongoing probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) are tracking the whereabouts of the late Tun Daim Zainuddin’s widow, Toh Puan Na’imah Abdul Khalid, and her children, who are believed to be in Europe, to assist in an ongoing police investigation. “At this stage, PDRM is actively working to locate the late Tun Daim’s widow, Toh Puan Na’imah Abdul Khalid, and her children to assist in completing the ongoing police investigation. Saifuddin Nasution said PDRM had opened an investigation paper following Sentul Police Report No.

items.title: 4WD slips off deteriorating Baram logging bridge, renewing concerns over rural road safety
items.summary: MIRI, July 3 — A near-tragic incident involving a four-wheel-drive (4WD) vehicle on a deteriorating logging bridge along the road to SK Long Pelutan has renewed concerns over poor road conditions in the Baram interior. The incident occurred on Wednesday when the vehicle reportedly slipped off while crossing a damaged bridge along the route linking Long Lama and Miri, a road frequently used by teachers travelling to and from SK Long Pelutan. “The road conditions at the scene are extremely poor and dangerous.

items.title: Ex‑kindergarten teacher charged with laundering RM133,550 linked to alleged child‑relocation scheme to New Zealand
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 3 — A former kindergarten teacher pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to three counts of money laundering involving RM133,550 in cash and gold jewellery allegedly linked to a scheme promising to facilitate a child’s relocation to New Zealand last year. According to the charges, Venmani is accused of receiving proceeds from unlawful activities in three separate transactions involving RM83,550, RM50,000, two gold chains and a gold pendant from a 39-year-old local woman. The charges were framed under Section 4(1)(b) of the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001 (Act 613), punishable under Section 4(1) of the same Act.

items.title: Anwar boosts taxi replacement fund by RM10m, says dedicated Proton S70 financing in the works
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today announced an additional RM10 million allocation for the Vehicle Replacement Matching Grant Programme to help taxi drivers replace ageing vehicles. Anwar, who is also the Finance Minister, said the additional funding follows the encouraging response to the initial RM10 million allocation for the National Madani Taxi Reform Programme, announced in Budget 2026. Given the importance of the taxi industry, I have approved an immediate additional RM10 million specifically for taxi reform,” he said at the launch of the National Madani Taxi Reform Programme at Dataran Merdeka today.

items.title: Armizan: Applications open today for diesel‑subsidy scheme for company jeeps, pick‑ups; payments start July 15
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 3 — Applications for the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS) have opened today for the land transport sector covering company-owned private vehicles, including jeeps and pick-up trucks, Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said. “To qualify, vehicles must be registered as business vehicles under the Company Private Use (AE) class/code in the Road Transport Department’s (JPJ) MySikap system. The government had previously extended SKDS eligibility to two other categories: the public land transport sector and the consumer goods land transport sector.

items.title: PLUS: Smart Lane at Bandar Saujana Putra-Putra Heights to close for five months for maintenance
items.summary: PLUS Malaysia Berhad (PLUS) in a statement today said the temporary closure is to facilitate maintenance works as part of its ongoing efforts to improve the highway infrastructure and ensure the safety of highway users. “Throughout the maintenance works, the existing three lanes at the affected stretch will remain open to maintain traffic flow and to minimise traffic disruptions for highway users,” the statement read. PLUS also encouraged highway users to plan their journeys using the PLUS app for the latest traffic updates and obey all traffic signage and instructions issued by PLUS personnel when passing through the construction zone.

items.title: Executor of late Waz Lian co-founder Ta Kin Yan’s estate says will is clear, leaves no room for dispute
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — The executor and trustee of the late Tan Sri Ta Kin Yan’s estate said the late businessman’s will is clear in its intent and leaves no room for dispute. Datuk Poh Po Lian, who co-founded the Waz Lian Group with Ta in 1987, said Ta’s will — which had been kept in a bank safe — provides a clear and equitable framework for administering the estate. The will, dated February 4, 2008, appoints Poh as the executor and trustee of Ta’s estate.

items.title: Foreign woman reported missing since June 30 found dead in Penampang apartment; police rule out foul play
items.summary: PENAMPANG, July 3 — A 42-year-old foreign woman who had been reported missing since June 30 was found dead in her apartment along Jalan Penampang Lama. Penampang police chief Supt Sammy Newton said the victim, who lived alone, was found by her employer who had become concerned when repeated attempts to contact her since that morning went unanswered. “Upon arriving at the residence, the employer found the apartment door locked and received no response from the victim, and proceeded to contact the authorities.

items.title: Anti-financial crime body says Azam Baki’s board role is in ‘individual capacity’, not tied to MACC
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — The National Financial Crime Prevention Centre (NFCC) today insisted that Tan Sri Azam Baki was appointed to its advisory board in his individual capacity, and not for his former position as MACC chief commissioner. “Therefore, his membership in the NFCC advisory board is not tied to the position of chief commissioner of the MACC and remains in force until the appointment expires, unless terminated earlier by the Yang di-Pertuan Agong,” the NFCC said. The NFCC also clarified that Azam’s term is valid for three years, running from September 2024 to September 2027.

items.title: Gempur Rasuah Sabah organisers confirm rally route, cite Federal Court ruling in decision not to submit police notice
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 3 — Organisers of the Gempur Rasuah Sabah 3.0 anti-corruption protest have confirmed that participants will assemble at Suria Sabah on Saturday before marching through the city centre to Dataran Todak Waterfront. In a statement released ahead of the event, the organisers announced that the gathering would begin at 2pm in front of Suria Sabah, followed by a procession passing several checkpoints including the Kota Kinabalu flyover, Centre Point, Anjung Kinabalu and Pasar Besar Kota Kinabalu before concluding at Dataran Todak Waterfront. The organisers also confirmed that they would not be submitting a notice of assembly to the police, citing a Federal Court decision delivered in June 2025 which declared Section 9(5) of the Peaceful Assembly Act 2012 unconstitutional.

items.title: Pakatan outlines vision for state with ‘Johor Untuk Semua’ manifesto
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 3 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) has unveiled its Johor election manifesto, ‘Johor Untuk Semua’, a strategic blueprint centred on modernising the state and securing its future. “The manifesto is not an election document, rather it is a roadmap to make Johor successful,” Maszlee said. Earlier in his speech, Amirudin said the PH manifesto offer was to elevate and empower Johor as a perfect and complete state.

items.title: Govt to build new Bandar Enstek hospital as MOH moves to ease HTJ congestion and expand services, says Dzulkefly
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — A new hospital will be built in Bandar Enstek, Nilai, Negeri Sembilan, to meet the healthcare needs of residents in the Seremban district while easing congestion at the Tuanku Ja’afar Hospital. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said the proposed hospital in the northern Seremban corridor was appropriate in view of the area’s rapid development, which had directly contributed to a sharp increase in population. He said the decision to build the new hospital followed the Health Ministry (MOH)’s review of the proposed Tuanku Ja’afar Hospital 2 (HTJ2) project in Rasah, as well as discussions with Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun on June 16.

items.title: High Court sets October trial dates for Nhaveen murder case following overturned acquittal
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 3 — The High Court here has scheduled eight trial dates in October for four men charged with the murder of teenager T Nhaveen nine years ago. Deputy Public Prosecutor Amril Johari confirmed that Judge Radzi Abdul Hamid will preside over the trial from October 7 to 9, and again from October 12 to 16. A case mention has been set for July 13, following proceedings conducted via Zoom today, Free Malaysia Today reported.

items.title: Johor polls: BN’s Aznan pledges to boost eco‑tourism, oil and gas sectors in Tanjung Surat
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, July 3 — The incumbent assemblyman for Tanjung Surat, Aznan Tamin, is determined to strengthen the eco-tourism and oil and gas sectors as key drivers of economic growth in the constituency if given the mandate again in the 16th Johor State Election. Aznan said Tanjung Surat’s strategic location near the Pengerang oil and gas industrial hub also provides opportunities to create more high-income jobs, especially for young people. N39: Tanjung Surat — 26,943 1.

items.title: Not just a camera: Anthony Loke explains the ‘ecosystem’ behind AwAS maintenance costs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — Transport Minister Anthony Loke has corrected claims regarding the maintenance costs of the Automated Awareness Safety System (AwAS), clarifying that the actual annual cost for a single camera unit is approximately RM112,800 and not the RM337,000 figure frequently cited. In a written parliamentary reply yesterday, Loke explained that the RM112,800 figure covers essential preventive maintenance, repairs, and periodic calibration. He said that the higher, commonly referenced total actually reflects the system’s entire operational ecosystem rather than the maintenance of individual cameras.

items.title: The future of campaigning? Johor election becomes Malaysian politics biggest generative AI test bed
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 3 — The Johor state election is becoming Malaysia’s biggest test bed for generative AI. As 2.7 million voters prepare to head to the polls on July 11, political teams from Pakatan Harapan (PH), Barisan Nasional (BN), and Parti Bersama Malaysia are increasingly replacing traditional production with AI-generated anthems, social media posts, and rally planning. Adib Aiman, a PH content creator who has generated hundreds of songs using the AI music generator Suno, noted the efficiency of the transition.
